name: gRPC Server Reflection exposes internal services in production
description: Probes the gRPC Server Reflection endpoint via its gRPC-Web HTTP/1.1 surface (so the scan can hit it without an HTTP/2 client). Vulnerable when reflection is enabled in production and returns a non-empty service list — leaks service catalogue + method signatures to anyone who can reach the port.

vulnerability.remediation: In ASP.NET Core, drop `app.MapGrpcReflectionService()` from the production build, OR gate it behind `.RequireAuthorization("Admin")`. Verify with `grpcurl -plaintext <host> list` — should return "Failed to list services: ...". Some incident-response runbooks ALWAYS treat reflection in prod as a finding regardless of authentication, since it widens the attack surface even for valid admin users.
